user.segment: English-speaking readers looking for a legitimate way to access a known book
user.scenario: They know a title, author, or ISBN and want a clear reading path within one minute

task.description: Find a legitimate download, borrow, preview, purchase, or local-library path for one book

primaryInput.name: Book query
primaryInput.constraints: 1 to 160 characters; ISBN-10 and ISBN-13 checksums validated in ISBN mode

primaryOutput.name: Reading path result
primaryOutput.format: book record with source, access status, and direct actions
primaryOutput.userAction: Download a public-domain file, borrow, preview, buy, or save the record locally

keywords: find where to read a book

monetization.paidSignal: Users click legitimate purchase routes or repeatedly use local shelf and export features

sources.name: Open Library Search and ISBN APIs
sources.license: Open Library terms and Internet Archive data policies
sources.quota: fair use; cache and identify production traffic

sources.name: Google Books Volumes API
sources.license: Google Books API Terms of Service

sources.name: Gutendex and Project Gutenberg
sources.license: Gutendex GPL for software; Project Gutenberg terms and territorial copyright rules for content
sources.quota: no SLA; self-host catalog for sustained traffic

sources.name: User-owned EPUB and PDF files
sources.quota: 50 MB per file in browser IndexedDB

data.fallback: Return successful sources, label unavailable sources, and keep local shelf features usable

mvp: multi-source title, author, and ISBN search
explicit public-domain, borrow, preview, purchase, and metadata-only states
nonGoals: hosting third-party public-domain bandwidth as a paid download service
